title: pouteria ciliolata
intro: Pouteria ciliolata, also known as the West Indian Mahogany, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Pouteria ciliolata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. Pouteria ciliolata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
